title: Set up Progressive Web App (PWA) in Next.js
summary: Add PWA support to Next.js using @ducanh2912/next-pwa: service worker, web manifest, offline fallback, and installable app experience.
declaration: I will add Progressive Web App support to this Next.js project using @ducanh2912/next-pwa (the actively maintained fork). This enables offline support, installability, and a service worker. If you are using Next.js 13 or below, say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set up Progressive Web App (PWA) in Next.js ### 1. Install dependencies - Run: ``` npm install @ducanh2912/next-pwa npm install -D webpack ``` - Do NOT install the original `next-pwa` package (shadowwalker) — it has not been updated for Next.js 14+ and is broken on App Router. ### 2. Configure next.config.ts - Wrap your Next.js config with the PWA plugin: ```ts import withPWAInit from '@ducanh2912/next-pwa'; const withPWA = withPWAInit({ dest: 'public', cacheOnFrontEndNav: true, aggressiveFrontEndNavCaching: true, reloadOnOnline: true, disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development', workboxOptions: { disableDevLogs: true, }, }); export default withPWA({ // your existing Next.js config here }); ``` - `disable: NODE_ENV === 'development'` prevents the service worker from caching dev bundles. ### 3. Add the Web App Manifest - Create `public/manifest.json`: ```json { "name": "My App", "short_name": "MyApp", "description": "My Progressive Web App", "start_url": "/", "display": "standalone", "background_color": "#ffffff", "theme_color": "#000000", "orientation": "portrait-primary", "icons": [ { "src": "/icons/icon-192x192.png", "sizes": "192x192", "type": "image/png" }, { "src": "/icons/icon-512x512.png", "sizes": "512x512", "type": "image/png" }, { "src": "/icons/icon-512x512.png", "sizes": "512x512", "type": "image/png", "purpose": "maskable" } ] } ``` - Create icons at `public/icons/icon-192x192.png` and `public/icons/icon-512x512.png`. - Use https://www.pwabuilder.com/imageGenerator to generate all icon sizes from a source image. ### 4. Add manifest metadata to layout.tsx - In `src/app/layout.tsx`, update the metadata export: ```ts import type { Metadata } from 'next'; export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'My App', description: 'My Progressive Web App', manifest: '/manifest.json', appleWebApp: { capable: true, statusBarStyle: 'default', title: 'My App', }, icons: { apple: '/icons/icon-192x192.png', }, }; ``` - The `manifest` field auto-generates the `` tag — do not add it manually. ### 5. Add theme-color meta tag - In `src/app/layout.tsx` inside the ``, add: ```tsx ``` - Or use Next.js 14's `viewport` export: ```ts export const viewport = { themeColor: '#000000' }; ``` ### 6. Create an offline fallback page - Create `src/app/~offline/page.tsx`: ```tsx export default function OfflinePage() { return (

You are offline

Please check your internet connection and try again.

); } ``` - `@ducanh2912/next-pwa` automatically routes to `/~offline` when the user navigates while offline. ### 7. Update .gitignore - Add PWA-generated files: ``` # PWA generated files public/workbox-*.js public/workbox-*.js.map public/sw.js public/sw.js.map public/fallback-*.js public/fallback-*.js.map ``` - These are generated at build time and must not be committed. ### 8. Build and verify - Run: `npm run build && npm run start` (test with `start`, not `dev` — PWA is disabled in development) - After build, verify `public/sw.js` and `public/workbox-*.js` exist. - In Chrome DevTools → Application → Service Workers: confirm SW is registered. - In Application → Manifest: confirm all fields populate correctly. - An install icon should appear in the browser address bar. ### 9. Test offline behavior - In Chrome DevTools → Network → throttle to Offline. - Reload the page — it should serve from cache or show the offline fallback. - Navigate to a previously-visited page to confirm it loads without network. ### Common errors & fixes - **`Cannot find module '@ducanh2912/next-pwa'`** — Not installed. Run `npm install @ducanh2912/next-pwa`. - **`TypeError: withPWAInit is not a function`** — Wrong import style. Use the default import: `import withPWAInit from '@ducanh2912/next-pwa'`. - **Service worker not registering in dev** — Expected. PWA is disabled in development. Test with `npm run build && npm run start`. - **`sw.js` missing after build** — `dest: 'public'` is absent in your config. Verify `next.config.ts`. - **Install prompt not appearing** — Malformed `manifest.json`. The manifest must have `name`, `short_name`, `start_url`, `display: 'standalone'`, and icons with both 192px and 512px variants. - **Icons not displaying** — PNG files don't exist at the declared paths. Check `public/icons/` directory. - **`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'tapAsync')`** — Webpack not installed. Run `npm install -D webpack`. - **Stale cached content after redeploy** — Force-unregister the service worker in DevTools → Application → Storage → Clear site data, then hard-reload. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT install `next-pwa` from `@shadowwalker/next-pwa` — it is unmaintained and broken on Next.js 14+. Always use `@ducanh2912/next-pwa`. - Do NOT enable PWA in development (`disable: false`) — the service worker will cache dev bundles and cause stale-content bugs that are very hard to debug. - Do NOT commit the generated `sw.js` and `workbox-*.js` files — add them to `.gitignore`. - Do NOT skip the offline fallback page — without it, navigating while offline shows a browser error page instead of your app. - Do NOT add `` manually in `` — use the Next.js `metadata.manifest` field instead. - Do NOT skip icon generation — Chrome requires at least 192px and 512px icons for installability. Missing icons will silently prevent the install prompt.
